content: Influenza diagnosis: 1) Rapid influenza diagnostic tests (RIDT) detect viral antigens in 10-15 minutes but have variable sensitivity (50-70%); 2) Rapid molecular assays (NAAT) provide results in 15-30 minutes with high sensitivity/specificity; 3) RT-PCR (gold standard) from nasopharyngeal swab, nasal aspirate, or bronchoalveolar lavage. Testing indicated during influenza season for hospitalized patients, high-risk individuals, and when results affect management. Surveillance specimens track circulating strains for vaccine formulation. Vaccine effectiveness varies annually (40-60% typical) based on strain match; quadrivalent vaccines cover two A strains (H1N1, H3N2) and two B lineages. Annual vaccination recommended for all ≥6 months unless contraindicated.
